author: bridge-bot
content: <potuz0> Hey @emile17 Sorry to keep banging on the same drum but I am still puzzled by your comment on the size of the ZK starks. I assume that your complain about the bandwidth necessary for validation in the scheme I mentioned is because of the ZK proof of correct generation that each attester needs to send to prove that they aren't equivocating in their message right? this is not about the PQ signature of that message in itself. Is this statement correct?

author: bridge-bot
content: <emile17> Correct, I would add that in your construction iiuc you don’t even need a real PQ signature like sphincs. Each validator could instead publicly register hash(secret_seed), and when a validator generates a ZK-STARK of correct derivation, it proves knowledge “in ZK” of the preimage of the hash (same idea as ziggy (https://x.com/EliBenSasson/status/1332057814131494917?s=20) actually)

author: bridge-bot
content:
<emile17> re: **@potuz0**
> Hey @emile17 Sorry to keep banging on the same drum but I am still puzzled by your comment on the size of the ZK starks. I assume that your complain about the bandwidth necessary for validation in the scheme I mentioned is because of the ZK proof of correct generation that each attester needs to send to prove that they aren't equivocating in their message right? this is not about the PQ signature of that message in itself. Is this statement correct?
One way to reduce bandwidth could be to generate many (like 100) BLS keypairs, instead of only one, per ZK-STARK?

author: bridge-bot
content:
<tcoratger> @potuz0 am I wrong if I say that your approach aims to limit the centralization due to the post quantum signature scheme with aggregation requiring a new aggregator special role?

If this is the case I really don't think it's a major problem because everything that we are doing right now for leanVM and the aggregation machinery is CPU based and so the aggregator role just requires you to buy a laptop (leanVM benchmarks are run by @emile17 on a M4 which is very far from the GPU clusters for real time block proving where the question of centralization can arise) or maybe I missed something in the conversation.
